title: ColourfulText uses fixed blue gradient, not theme-aware
description: ColourfulText.tsx line 44 uses hardcoded gradient classes: from-blue-600 via-blue-500 to-blue-700. These bypass the site's teal/primary token system entirely. In dark mode, the blue gradient may have poor contrast against the dark background and feels off-brand relative to the rest of the palette. Fix: Either use primary token colors for the gradient or add dark: variants with appropriate contrast.
close_reason: Made the site light-only by adding color-scheme: light, removing the dark mode CSS variable block, removing all dark: class variants, and removing dark-mode glow effects.

title: Post-Launch Follow-ups (Epic)
description: Follow-up items from PRD section 16: FAQ rewrite, public asset reordering, score-as-brand-object strategy.

title: Make Navbar server-first to remove global Framer Motion tax
description: CRITICAL: Navbar is in layout.tsx and uses 'use client' + Framer Motion, so every single page pays the hydration + bundle cost. The slide-in animation could use CSS transitions/animations instead, or the motion wrapper should be isolated into a tiny client component wrapping a server-rendered NavbarShell.

title: Fix blog slug/id lookup mismatch causing 404s
description: HIGH: In src/app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx, generateStaticParams falls back to p.id when no slug exists, but findPostBySlug() in content.ts only matches on p.slug. Posts without slugs will generate params but return 404. Fix: update findPostBySlug to also match on id, or create findPostBySlugOrId().

title: Restructure pages as Server Components
description: CRITICAL: server-serialization rule violation. about/page.tsx and talks/page.tsx are entirely client-rendered via 'use client', shipping all static content (large HTML strings, founder bios, advisor data, talk descriptions) as JavaScript. These should be Server Components with small client subcomponents extracted only for interactive parts (animations, expand/collapse).
close_reason: Already resolved by referee-idc: about and talks pages converted to server components with CSS animations

title: Dark accent colors not defined in dark mode palette
description: The dark mode CSS variable block in globals.css (lines 56-82) redefines primary, border, card, and nav tokens but does NOT redefine accent colors (--accent-700 through --accent-300). This means accent colors like text-accent-600 (used for error messages in NewsletterSignup.tsx:89) remain their light-mode values in dark mode, causing poor contrast against dark backgrounds. Fix: Add dark-tuned accent color variables to the dark mode media query block.
close_reason: Made the site light-only by adding color-scheme: light, removing the dark mode CSS variable block, removing all dark: class variants, and removing dark-mode glow effects.

title: Implement Content-Signal headers
Light-mode tint classes used across components without dark alternatives
description:
Multiple components use bg-primary-50/50, hover:bg-primary-50, border-primary-200 which in dark mode become very dark teal fills (because primary shades are semantically inverted). These look muddy, sunken, and have poor separation from surrounding surfaces. Affected files and lines:
- src/app/page.tsx:80 (solution card: border-primary-200 bg-primary-50/50)
- src/app/page.tsx:203 (book-demo section: bg-primary-50/50)
- src/components/Hero.tsx:63 (secondary CTA: hover:bg-primary-50)
- src/app/about/page.tsx (mission block, bottom CTA, LinkedIn hover)
- src/app/blog/page.tsx (latest badge: border-primary-200 bg-primary-50)
- src/app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx (back button hover, footer CTA, blockquotes)
- src/components/ExpandableSummary.tsx:32 (hover:bg-primary-50/50)
Fix: Create semantic tokens like --callout-bg, --callout-border, --outline-hover that have proper values in both light and dark modes, then replace the hardcoded primary shade classes.
close_reason: Made the site light-only by adding color-scheme: light, removing the dark mode CSS variable block, removing all dark: class variants, and removing dark-mode glow effects.

title: Update Hero/Aurora background for dark mode
description: Fix hardcoded bg-slate-50 and text colors
close_reason: Updated with semantic colors and dark mode blend modes

title: Aurora background effect reduces contrast in dark mode
description: aurora-background.tsx lines 28-43 use mix-blend-screen in dark mode (dark:after:mix-blend-screen) with bright teal gradients and blur effects. This can make Hero and PageIntro backgrounds look washed out or noisy behind text, reducing readability. The opacity is reduced to 20% in dark mode (dark:opacity-20 on line 41) but the blend mode still produces visual artifacts. Consumers affected: Hero.tsx and PageIntro.tsx. Fix: Consider a different blend mode for dark mode, further reduce opacity, or use a simpler gradient treatment.
close_reason: Made the site light-only by adding color-scheme: light, removing the dark mode CSS variable block, removing all dark: class variants, and removing dark-mode glow effects.

title: IMMEDIATE: Update patch-version dependencies
description:

- tailwindcss: 4.1.13 → 4.1.18
- framer-motion: 12.23.12 → 12.23.26
- eslint: 9.35.0 → 9.39.2

- @types/react-dom: 19.1.9 → 19.2.3
- eslint-plugin-react-hooks: 6.1.0 → 7.0.1

Risk: Low - all are patch/minor updates
Should complete: This week
close_reason: All dependencies updated successfully. Lint and build passed.

title: HIGH: Remove duplicate motion package
description:
Both framer-motion and motion packages installed (v12.23.12)
All code uses framer-motion - motion is unused

Impact: ~100KB bundle bloat
Action: pnpm remove motion
close_reason: Duplicate motion package removed. ~100KB bundle reduction. All tests passed.

title: CMS content contains hardcoded light-mode inline styles
description:
content/content.json contains legacy WordPress/CMS HTML with inline styles that bypass theme tokens in dark mode:
- Hardcoded text colors: color:#333333, color:#0d0d0d
- Hardcoded backgrounds: background-color:#ffffff
- Embedded <style> blocks (Elementor CSS)
- Inline font-family overrides
Blog post renderer (src/app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx) already strips style attributes via sanitize-html, mitigating this for blog posts. However, ExpandableSummary.tsx (line 58) renders HTML via dangerouslySetInnerHTML without sanitization, making it vulnerable if styled HTML is ever passed. Fix: (1) Ensure all HTML rendering paths strip inline styles. (2) Consider cleaning content.json source data.
close_reason: Made the site light-only by adding color-scheme: light, removing the dark mode CSS variable block, removing all dark: class variants, and removing dark-mode glow effects.

title: Update hero tagline to 'Adding quality and nuance to the scholarly record'
Fix nested main elements in layout and pages
description: HIGH: layout.tsx wraps children in a main element, but page.tsx and talks/page.tsx also render main as their root element, creating invalid nested main landmarks. Only one main element should exist per document. Fix: keep main in layout.tsx, change pages to use div or section.

description: PRD §6, §11, §13. Implement copy system: preferred terms (transparent reliability score, scholarly record, inspectable, etc.), banned hero terms (founder-led, pre-seed, etc.), precision guardrails on 300+ claims, tone (clinical/serious/technical/restrained).

title: NewsletterSignup component has no dark mode styling
description:
NewsletterSignup.tsx is designed entirely for light mode. Issues:
- Line 52: bg-gradient-to-br from-primary-50 to-primary-100/50 becomes a very dark gradient in dark mode
- Line 52: border-primary-200 becomes a dark teal border with poor contrast
- Line 64: Success box uses bg-primary-50 border-primary-300 which becomes dark/muddy
- Line 89: Error text uses text-accent-600 which has no dark mode definition
The whole component needs bespoke dark mode treatment with either dark: variants or semantic tokens.
close_reason: Made the site light-only by adding color-scheme: light, removing the dark mode CSS variable block, removing all dark: class variants, and removing dark-mode glow effects.

title: Redesign website for dark mode and modern aesthetics
close_reason: Complete redesign with teal/amber palette, Sora font, and full dark mode support

title: Create /llms-full.txt with comprehensive content
Add SPA navigation tracking to Google Analytics
description: MEDIUM: src/components/GoogleAnalytics.tsx only tracks initial page load via window.location.pathname in the initial gtag config call. App Router SPA navigations are not tracked. Fix: add a client hook using usePathname() from next/navigation that calls gtag config on route changes.

title: MEDIUM-TERM: Setup automated dependency monitoring
description:
Prevent future dependency drift with automated monitoring.

Options:
1. Dependabot (GitHub native)
   - Add .github/dependabot.yml
   - Configure update schedule
   - Set grouping rules

2. Renovate (more configurable)

   - Better monorepo support

Recommended: Dependabot for simplicity

Configuration:

- Monthly dependency updates
- Group patch updates together
- Auto-merge patch versions with passing tests

Timeline: Next quarter

title: Missing color-scheme and html dark-mode theming
description: The html element has no color-scheme property and no dark background/color styling. Only body gets themed. This causes: (1) Browser form controls, scrollbars, and built-in UI remain light in dark mode. (2) White flashes on load/overscroll because html background is unset. Fix: Add color-scheme: light to html, switch to color-scheme: dark in prefers-color-scheme media query, and apply background/color vars to html element. Files: src/app/globals.css lines 151-155 (html rule), lines 140-142 (body rule).

title: SHORT-TERM: Plan Next.js 16 migration strategy
description:
# Next.js 15.5.7 → 16.1.1 Migration Plan

## Executive Summary
**Status**: ✅ **EXCELLENT NEWS** - This codebase is already 95% compatible with Next.js 16!

**Migration Complexity**: LOW
**Estimated Time**: 1-2 hours (mostly testing)
**Risk Level**: LOW

## Analysis Results

### ✅ What Already Works in Next.js 16

1. **Async params pattern** - src/app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx:14-27
   - Already using `params: Promise<{ slug: string }>` with `await params`
   - This is the REQUIRED Next.js 16 pattern - no changes needed!

2. **No middleware** - No custom middleware.ts to rename to proxy.ts

3. **Minimal next.config.ts** - No webpack or experimental.turbopack config
   - Will automatically use Turbopack in v16 (can opt-out with --webpack flag)

4. **No breaking API usage**:
   - ✅ No searchParams
   - ✅ No cookies()
   - ✅ No headers()
   - ✅ No draftMode()
   - ✅ No image generation APIs
   - ✅ No AMP
   - ✅ No runtime config
   - ✅ No parallel routes

5. **next/image usage** - Standard usage in Navbar, About, Home
   - Using modern next/image (not legacy)
   - No query strings or deprecated patterns

6. **Node.js runtime** - Implicitly using nodejs runtime (fs usage in lib/content.ts)
   - No edge runtime conflicts

### 🔧 Required Changes

#### 1. Update Dependencies (package.json)
```json
{
  "dependencies": {
    "next": "16.1.1",  // from 15.5.7
    "react": "19.2.3",  // already correct
    "react-dom": "19.2.3"  // already correct
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "eslint-config-next": "16.1.1",  // from 15.5.7
    "@types/react": "^19.2.7",  // already correct
    "@types/react-dom": "^19.2.3"  // already correct
  }
}
```

#### 2. Optional: Explicit Runtime Declaration
**Files**: src/app/blog/page.tsx, src/app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx
**Why**: Prevent accidental edge runtime usage (fs requires nodejs)
**Change**: Add to both files
```ts
export const runtime = "nodejs";
```

#### 3. Optional: Strict Static Params
**File**: src/app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx
**Why**: 404 unknown slugs at routing layer
**Change**: Add export
```ts
export const dynamicParams = false;
```

## Migration Steps

### Step 1: Dependency Update
```bash
# Update Next.js and related packages
pnpm update next@16.1.1 eslint-config-next@16.1.1

# Verify lockfile
pnpm install
```

### Step 2: Run Automated Codemod (Optional)
```bash
# Next.js provides migration codemod
npx @next/codemod@canary upgrade latest

# This checks for:
# - Middleware → proxy renames (none in our case)
# - Runtime config removal (none in our case)
# - Turbopack config migration (none in our case)
```

### Step 3: Build & Verify
```bash
# Clean build
rm -rf .next

# Build with Next.js 16
pnpm build

# Expected: No errors, all pages generated
```

### Step 4: Dev Server Testing
```bash
pnpm dev

# Test routes:
# - / (home)
# - /about
# - /blog
# - /blog/[any-post-slug]
# - /faq
# - /talks
```

### Step 5: Verify Features
- [ ] Static generation works (check .next/server/pages)
- [ ] Blog posts render with sanitized HTML
- [ ] Images load (next/image optimization)
- [ ] Google Analytics loads
- [ ] Framer Motion animations work
- [ ] Content.json loaded correctly
- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors

## Testing Strategy

### Build-Time Tests
```bash
pnpm build
# Check: No deprecation warnings
# Check: All blog posts in build output
```

### Runtime Tests
```bash
pnpm dev
# Visit: /, /about, /blog, /blog/first-post, /faq, /talks
# Check: No console errors
# Check: Images render
# Check: Navigation works
# Check: GA scripts load
```

### Linting
```bash
pnpm lint
# Expect: No new ESLint errors from Next.js 16 rules
```

## Deployment Considerations

### Vercel
1. **Node.js Version**: Verify Next.js 16 Node requirements
   - Check: https://nextjs.org/docs/app/guides/upgrading/version-16#system-requirements
   - Update vercel.json if needed: `"engines": { "node": ">=20.9.0" }`

2. **Build Command**: No changes needed
   - `pnpm build` works as-is

3. **Environment Variables**: Verify
   - `NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_MEASUREMENT_ID` still set

### Standalone Output (Future)
If migrating to standalone later:
```ts
// next.config.ts
const nextConfig: NextConfig = {
  output: "standalone",
  outputFileTracingIncludes: {
    "/": ["./content/content.json"],
  },
};
```

## Breaking Changes Reference

From official Next.js 16 upgrade guide:

1. ✅ **Async Request APIs** - Already implemented correctly
2. ✅ **Middleware → Proxy** - No middleware in project
3. ✅ **Turbopack Default** - No custom webpack config
4. ✅ **Parallel Routes** - Not using parallel routes
5. ✅ **Image Changes** - Using standard patterns
6. ✅ **Removed Features** - Not using AMP, next lint, or runtime config

## Timeline

**Week 1**:
- Day 1: Update dependencies, run build, test locally
- Day 2: Deploy to staging/preview, verify production behavior
- Day 3: Monitor, then promote to production

**Rollback Plan**:
```bash
# If issues found, revert package.json and reinstall
git checkout HEAD -- package.json pnpm-lock.yaml
pnpm install
```

## Documentation Links

- Official Next.js 16 Upgrade Guide: https://nextjs.org/docs/app/guides/upgrading/version-16
- Next.js 16 Blog Post: https://nextjs.org/blog/next-16
- Breaking Changes Codemod: npx @next/codemod@canary upgrade latest

## Risk Assessment

**Overall Risk**: 🟢 LOW

**Confidence Level**: HIGH - Only dependency updates needed, no code changes required for core functionality.

**Recommended Approach**: Progressive rollout
1. Test locally ✓
2. Deploy to preview environment ✓
3. Monitor for 24 hours ✓
4. Promote to production ✓
close_reason: Migration plan completed. Codebase is already 95% Next.js 16 compatible. Only dependency updates needed (next@16.1.1, eslint-config-next@16.1.1). Comprehensive plan documented with testing strategy and deployment considerations.

title: Create /llms.txt with site description, key pages, and content categories
Use ternary over && for conditional rendering
description: LOW: rendering-conditional-render rule. Multiple components use && for conditional rendering (e.g., NewsletterSignup). While safe in current code since values are strings/booleans, the Vercel best practice recommends ternary operators to avoid accidentally rendering falsy values. Convert pattern: {cond && <X/>} to {cond ? <X/> : null}.

title: Visual/UX direction implementation
description: PRD §12. Sharp/editorial/technical/calm feel. Restrained palette, high contrast, subtle accent, minimal decorative gradients. Real product artifacts (scored paper cards, taxonomy chips, etc.). No stock photos, fake logos, or AI network art.

title: Add JSON-LD structured data (Organization, Article, FAQ)
Follow-up: Reorder public assets around product
description: PRD §16.2. Marketing stack order: landing → demo → about → technical overview → FAQ → talks → blog. Current site inverts this.

title: Build shared component set
description: PRD §14.3. Components: hero, score anatomy module, proof-card grid, process steps, audience card grid, honesty/status callout, founder teaser, CTA block. Reusable across landing+about pages.

title: Accessibility and responsive audit
description: PRD §14.2. Ensure responsive design, keyboard-accessible interactions, semantic headings, alt text, fast loading, no giant autoplay video.

title: Hoist static objects outside client components
description: MEDIUM: rerender-memo-with-default-value / rendering-hoist-jsx rule violation. Static objects like animation variants, founder data, and advisor arrays are created inside client components on every render in about/page.tsx and ColourfulText.tsx. These should be hoisted to module scope to avoid unnecessary re-allocations.
